manualPage.pageSub: Reference guide: 4 service types · pipeline stages · how to handle 8 special situations
manualPage.section3Title: 8 special situations — how to handle them
manualPage.ciDesc: Customer brings the device to the center themselves. Repaired at the center, then returned.
manualPage.stDesc: Company/warehouse device. Repaired at the center.
manualPage.ihDesc: Technician goes to repair at home. Device never comes to the center — finished on site.
manualPage.psName: Pick up device to repair at the center
manualPage.psDesc: Pick up device at customer's home → repair at center → return.
manualPage.pipelineIntroA: Every repair ticket flows through stages 1–12. PS and IH have a
manualPage.pipelineIntroB: of their own before entering stage 1.
manualPage.stageNoteB: (quotation) only happen when out of warranty · stages
manualPage.stageNoteC: (parts) only happen when parts are needed. In warranty + no parts → skip to stage 8.
manualPage.ihFlowB: (CS, must enter appointment date) → tech goes (check-in GPS) → inspect + repair → QC → close (no “return”)
manualPage.sit1Title: IH can't be repaired on site → bring into center
manualPage.sit1Sit: Technician arrived at the home but can't repair on site (needs center tools / deeper inspection).
manualPage.sit1Step1: Technician taps “Can't repair on site — bring into center” in the app (during stage 1–2) with a reason
manualPage.sit1Step2: The job converts to PS automatically → enters the “picking up” queue
manualPage.sit1Step3: CS taps “Receive into center” when the device arrives → inspect + repair at center → return
manualPage.sit1Note: Once it becomes PS, it automatically gets a “return” stage (the device must be delivered back to the customer).
manualPage.sit2Title: IH needs to order parts (no stock)
manualPage.sit2Sit: Inspection found that a part not in stock is needed. Decide during inspection:
manualPage.sit2Step1: Want to repair at center → tap “Bring into center” first (becomes PS) → order per the flow → part arrives → repair at center
manualPage.sit2Step2: Want to repair on site → don't bring in, order the part → part arrives (stage 8) → schedule the tech for a second visit
manualPage.sit2Note: The purchasing mechanism (stage 7) works the same — the only difference is whether it's brought into the center or a second visit.
manualPage.sit3Sit: The person who inspects and the person who will repair are different people.
manualPage.sit3Step2: CS/Supervisor opens the ticket at /service/<number> → taps “Change technician” to B
manualPage.sit3Step3: Technician B accepts the job (in the app) and continues the repair
manualPage.sit3Note: You can't change technician after parts have been requested (the request was issued under A). Labor pay goes to whoever closes the job (B).
manualPage.sit4Sit: Started the repair (stage 9) and found you need more parts or the wrong one. The job stays in “repairing”.
manualPage.sit4Step1: Web: on /service/<number>, in the “parts during repair” box — search + add / remove
manualPage.sit4Step2: App: the “Request / swap parts (during repair)” button
manualPage.sit4Step3: Tap “Request more parts” → issue a second parts request → warehouse issues them
manualPage.sit4Note: Swapping a part = return the old one + add the new one. A row already issued (locked) can't be removed.
manualPage.sit5Title: Cancel a job with outstanding parts
manualPage.sit5Sit: You want to close the job as cancelled, but parts issued for use (inspection/repair) haven't been returned.
manualPage.sit5Step1: The system won't let you close for free if there are outstanding parts (shows a warning)
manualPage.sit5Step2: Choose 1 of 2: return the parts (parts recovery page) or issue a receipt to charge for the parts
manualPage.sit5Step3: Then you can close the job / return the device to the customer
manualPage.sit5Note: Closes an old leak: a cancellation closed while parts were never returned ⇒ stock ends up lower than reality.
manualPage.sit6Title: Transfer parts to the repair room (no issue)
manualPage.sit6Sit: You want the parts at the repair room but still as stock (not deducted/not billed to a job).
manualPage.sit6Step1: Go to the Warehouse menu → “Request transfer of parts to repair room”
manualPage.sit6Step2: Choose the repair-room warehouse (1104 Khua Luang / 1206 Don Tiu) + search/add parts → create a transfer request
manualPage.sit6Step3: The main warehouse issues the actual transfer (FT) in ERP → tap “Receive” at Transfer tracking
manualPage.sit6Note: A transfer request doesn't deduct stock — it's a request for the main warehouse to transfer. Different from an “issue”, which deducts stock to a job.
manualPage.sit7Sit: You want to try fitting a part to diagnose (may or may not actually use it).
manualPage.sit7Step1: During inspection, add the part to the list and try it
manualPage.sit7Step2: Actually used → keep it on the list (proceed to issue)
manualPage.sit7Step3: Not used → remove it (before issuing) or return to warehouse (after issuing)
manualPage.sit7Note: Use the existing add/remove/return mechanism — no special marking needed.
manualPage.sit8Sit: Count the customer devices that should actually be in the center (stages 1–11).
manualPage.sit8Step1: Open the Warehouse menu → Stock count (web = table · mobile = scan)
manualPage.sit8Step2: Scan the device's barcode / SN — once found it's marked automatically
manualPage.sit8Note: IH is skipped (at the customer's home) · PS is counted only once received into the center.
manualPage.role1Duty: Receive into center (PS) · set appointment + assign tech (IH) · change tech · cancel · issue receipt
manualPage.role2Duty: Accept job · inspect · check-in/out GPS · repair · bring into center · request/swap parts
manualPage.role4Duty: Issue parts · transfer to repair room · receive transfers · receive returned parts · track inventory
manualPage.role5Duty: Pick up device at customer's home (PS) · deliver device back

sparePartsNew.heading: Request new spare code
sparePartsNew.notInStock: Not in stock yet — click “Pull new spare”

roleControls.revertToPositionRole: — it will revert to the position role
roleControls.canLoginAgain: will be able to log in again
roleControls.andNoNotify: and will no longer receive notifications

holdButtons.releaseTitle: Release — job returns to normal queue, clock resumes
holdButtons.releaseAskBody: returns to normal queue, clock resumes from where it stopped
holdButtons.cancelConfirm: will enter the "Approve cancellation" queue — an authorized approver must approve before it is actually cancelled
holdButtons.repairedConfirm: will be marked **repair complete** and move to the QC/return stage (skipping pending parts requisition)
holdButtons.verifyConfirm: will be marked "Verify still present" — clock stops, job moves to the ‘Needs check’ tab

receivePickupButton.dispatchMsgTail: at the customer's home — the job moves to "On the way to pick up"
receivePickupButton.undoDispatchMsgTail: will return to "Waiting to pick up" (not yet departed)
receivePickupButton.receiveMsgTail: and will move to "Waiting for inspection" — do this only when the courier has actually delivered the item

locationPicker.shortLinkNoCoords: Short link has no coordinates — open it, right-click the pin, then copy the coordinates and paste them here
locationPicker.coordsNotFound: No coordinates found in this text
locationPicker.pastePlaceholder: Paste a Google Maps link or coordinates (17.9757, 102.6331)
locationPicker.dialogSubtitle: Tap the map to drop a pin — the technician will navigate to this point

spareRecoveryDetail.subtitle: Parts that were issued out but the job was cancelled — recover them to the warehouse
spareRecoveryDetail.title: Outstanding parts · Receipt
spareRecoveryDetail.allRecovered: All parts on this receipt have been recovered

requestsRoworder.title: Spare request
requestsRoworder.subtitle: Select warehouse · Check balance · Confirm items before submitting
requestsRoworder.purchaseNeedSuffix: items. After receiving into stock, come back to create the request.
requestsRoworder.requestedNote: Spares already on an earlier request won't be added to the new one — track status on the "Spare requests" page
requestsRoworder.emptyNoSpares: No spares in the list yet — click "Select" to add spares
requestsRoworder.allRequested: All spares for this job have been requested or issued — to add more, click "Select"

approvalsPurchaseRequests.heading: Approve Purchase Requests
approvalsPurchaseRequests.subtitle: Purchase requests (SPR) in ERP · approving issues a WPRA + purchase order (PO) automatically

repairActions.undoStartRepairTail: and the timer will stop. Parts and issued withdrawal slips remain unchanged.

undoButtons.undoStartMsg: will return to "Waiting for installer" and stop the timer. Parts and issued slips already created remain unchanged.
undoButtons.undoFinishMsg: will return to "Installing". Cannot undo if the customer has already answered the survey.
undoButtons.reopenMsg: will return to "Waiting to close job". The customer's survey answers remain unchanged.

qcForm.failReasonPlaceholder: Reason for failing — the technician will see this message
qcForm.qcPassNext: QC passed — proceed to next step

quoteRowActions.removeMessageTail: — the item will return to awaiting a new quotation, and you cannot restore the old one!
quoteRowActions.deleteAndCreateNewTitle: Delete quotation and create a new one

installSpareRequests.title: Awaiting Spare Parts Request
installSpareRequests.searchPlaceholder: Search by no., bill no., customer, technician, item...
installSpareRequests.blockedNotice: There are still jobs using spare parts not yet in this queue:
installSpareRequests.jobsNotAssigned: jobs not yet assigned to a technician
installSpareRequests.jobsNotAccepted: jobs the technician has not yet accepted
installSpareRequests.mustAcceptFirst: The technician must accept the job before requesting spare parts

checkActions.startCheckMsgTail: will be moved to "Checking" and the timer will start
checkActions.undoStartMsgTail: will return to "Awaiting check" and the timer will stop
checkActions.cancelCheckMsgTail: (the symptoms the technician diagnosed and the selected parts) will be cleared and the job will return to "Checking" to be recorded again. The selected parts will be returned to the cart automatically.

scheduleRepairVisit.dialogHint: After the customer approves the price — set the date the technician will visit to repair

repeatRepairs.title: Repeat Repairs
repeatRepairs.subSuffix: days — not fixed on the first attempt
repeatRepairs.empty: No repeat repairs in this period

quotationEdit.agreedPriceLocked: this quotation — an agreed price cannot be edited afterwards
quotationEdit.reallyNeedEdit: If you really need to edit, go to
quotationEdit.thenWithdraw: then click “Withdraw response” first (cannot withdraw once a receipt is issued)
quotationEdit.editThenSave: Edit the price/items then save — the quotation will be resubmitted for approval automatically
quotationEdit.approvedInternallyTitle: This quotation is already approved internally
quotationEdit.approvedInternallyNote: If you save changes, the existing approval will be canceled and re-approval is required

stockBalanceRepair.title: Repair Stock Balance (Service Center)
stockBalanceRepair.subtitle: Spare parts in stock at repair warehouse 1104 (Khoua Luang) · 1206 (Don Tiw)
stockBalanceRepair.noDataYet: No data yet — click "Refresh from ERP" above first (takes ~25 seconds)

qcDetail.title: Quality Control
qcDetail.noChecklist: The checklist for this workflow has not been set up yet — QC cannot be passed until a manager sets up the items first.

installDispatch.notEnoughStock: Insufficient quantity to dispatch spare parts!
installDispatch.erpNoticeAfter: has been sent to ERP — dispatch from the warehouse in ERP referencing this document number. Once dispatched, the system will
installDispatch.erpNoticePull: pull the dispatch note back automatically
installDispatch.erpNoticeEnd: and the job will move to the "Receive Spares" stage.

serviceButtons.docsDeletedText: · Quotation · Requisition · Withdrawal · Receipt of this document
serviceButtons.stockBold: ERP stock already deducted will not return
serviceButtons.stockText: — parts disappear from the warehouse with no record
serviceButtons.keepHistoryHint: · Want the job out of the queue but keep its history ⇒ use "Cancel job" instead
serviceButtons.outstandingSuffix: baht · partial accepted (type the actual amount)

stockCountReport.title: Stock Count Result Report
stockCountReport.returnedTooltip: Already returned to customer in system but was counted
stockCountReport.confirmCloseMsg: Mark {code} as not-found (missing) — removes it from the count queue. Reversible later (no data deleted).
stockCountReport.confirmRestoreMsg: Return {code} to not-counted — you can count or re-mark it.

sparePicker.title: Select Spare Part (from ERP)
sparePicker.searchPlaceholder: Type code / name / brand of spare part...
sparePicker.pickHint: Click a row to select (multiple selectable)

installDeleteButton.docsDeletedText: · Requisition · Withdrawal · Parts receipt of this job
installDeleteButton.stockBold: ERP stock already deducted will not return
installDeleteButton.stockText: — parts disappear from the warehouse with no record
installDeleteButton.keepHistoryHint: · Want the job out of the queue but keep its history ⇒ use "Cancel job" instead

receiveReturnsDetail.title: Receive Spare Parts Return
receiveReturnsDetail.subtitle: Spare Parts Return List
receiveReturnsDetail.noSpares: No spare parts in this document
